ItacPersonItem.Notes: The son of Ferrante. Received a degree in both canon and civil law, his memebership as Linceo was proposed by Cesi in 1616 after the publication of Imperato`s book on fossils. See D. Busolini, Imperato, Francesco`, in DBI
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
